document_metadata.first_words: # Albian Ajeti: West Ham sign Basel striker for £8m - Published - comments West Ham have signed striker Albian Ajeti from Swiss side Basel for £8m. Ajeti, 22, scored 15 goals in 35 games last season and was part of the Switzerland squad that competed in the Nations League tournament in June. "I didn't have to think twice about signing for such a big, traditional club in the Premier League," he said after agreeing a four-year deal. West Ham have already spent a club record £45m on former France Under-21 forward Sebastien Haller this

chunks.content: West Ham have signed striker Albian Ajeti from Swiss side Basel for £8m. Ajeti, 22, scored 15 goals in 35 games last season and was part of the Switzerland squad that competed in the Nations League tournament in June. “I didn't have to think twice about signing for such a big, traditional club in the Premier League,“ he said after agreeing a four-year deal. West Ham have already spent a club record £45m on former France Under-21 forward Sebastien Haller this summer.
chunks.content_original: West Ham have signed striker Albian Ajeti from Swiss side Basel for £8m. Ajeti, 22, scored 15 goals in 35 games last season and was part of the Switzerland squad that competed in the Nations League tournament in June. “I didn't have to think twice about signing for such a big, traditional club in the Premier League,“ he said after agreeing a four-year deal. West Ham have already spent a club record £45m on former France Under-21 forward Sebastien Haller this summer.

chunks.content: Ajeti is the Hammers' sixth signing, following the arrivals of Haller, midfielder Pablo Fornals, defender Goncalo Cardosa and goalkeepers Roberto and David Martin. It is anticipated Mexican striker Javier Hernandez will remain at London Stadium, while Andy Carroll has left. Youngster Grady Diangana has joined West Bromwich Albion on a season-long loan. The 21-year-old made 17 Premier League appearances last season, but has now linked up with former Hammers manager Slaven Bilic at The Hawthorns. Find all the latest football transfers on our dedicated page.

It is anticipated Mexican striker Javier Hernandez will remain at London Stadium, while Andy Carroll has left. Youngster Grady Diangana has joined West Bromwich Albion on a season-long loan. The 21-year-old made 17 Premier League appearances last season, but has now linked up with former Hammers manager Slaven Bilic at The Hawthorns. # Albian Ajeti: West Ham sign Basel striker for £8m

- Published8 August 2019
- comments[158 Comments](#comments)
Shareclose panelShare pageCopy link[About sharing](https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms/can-i-share-things-from-the-bbc)![Albian Ajeti](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/624/cpsprodpb/48F0/production/_108227681_albianajeti2.jpg)Image source, Getty ImagesImage caption, 

Albian Ajeti has six caps for Switzerland Under-21s

**West Ham have signed striker Albian Ajeti from Swiss side Basel for £8m.**

Ajeti, 22, scored 15 goals in 35 games last season and was part of the Switzerland squad that competed in the Nations League tournament in June.

"I didn't have to think twice about signing for such a big, traditional club in the Premier League," he said after agreeing a four-year deal.

West Ham have already spent a [club record £45m](/sport/football/49017405) on former France Under-21 forward Sebastien Haller this summer.

- [Live: Transfer deadline day - all the latest moves](/sport/live/football/49195032)

Ajeti is the Hammers' sixth signing, following the arrivals of Haller, midfielder Pablo Fornals, defender Goncalo Cardosa and goalkeepers Roberto and David Martin.

It is anticipated Mexican striker Javier Hernandez will remain at London Stadium, while [Andy Carroll has left.](/sport/football/48449380)

Sorry, we can't display this part of the article any more.

Youngster [Grady Diangana has joined West Bromwich Albion](/sport/football/49266483) on a season-long loan.

The 21-year-old made 17 Premier League appearances last season, but has now linked up with former Hammers manager Slaven Bilic at The Hawthorns.

**Find all the latest football transfers** [on our dedicated page.](/sport/football/transfers/)

Albian Ajeti has six caps for Switzerland Under-21s

**West Ham have signed striker Albian Ajeti from Swiss side Basel for £8m.**

Ajeti, 22, scored 15 goals in 35 games last season and was part of the Switzerland squad that competed in the Nations League tournament in June.

"I didn't have to think twice about signing for such a big, traditional club in the Premier League," he said after agreeing a four-year deal.

West Ham have already spent a [club record £45m](/sport/football/49017405) on former France Under-21 forward Sebastien Haller this summer.

- [Live: Transfer deadline day - all the latest moves](/sport/live/football/49195032)

Ajeti is the Hammers' sixth signing, following the arrivals of Haller, midfielder Pablo Fornals, defender Goncalo Cardosa and goalkeepers Roberto and David Martin.

It is anticipated Mexican striker Javier Hernandez will remain at London Stadium, while [Andy Carroll has left.](/sport/football/48449380)

Sorry, we can't display this part of the article any more.

Youngster [Grady Diangana has joined West Bromwich Albion](/sport/football/49266483) on a season-long loan.

The 21-year-old made 17 Premier League appearances last season, but has now linked up with former Hammers manager Slaven Bilic at The Hawthorns.
